methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Lumina Vehicles is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ahmed Shubber and Federico Di Palma. The comparable universe in Robotics & Manufacturing spans 9 peers: 1 public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. Despite identifying 11 same-sector peers, none satisfied the filtering criteria for narrow comparability: zero companies matched the seed stage, zero occupied adjacent development stages, and zero fell within a similar valuation tier. Applying an all-sector median would conflate different risk profiles and capital requirements across heterogeneous robotics subsegments, introducing material distortion.

Without a sufficiently dense peer set at the seed stage and valuation tier, a triangulated valuation range cannot be derived from market multiples. Alternative methods—such as venture capital method, discounted cash flow, or cost-of-capital approaches—would be required to establish a defensible range. The key uncertainty is the lack of granular pricing data for seed-stage Robotics & Manufacturing firms. Any valuation recommendation should acknowledge that comparable-company analysis alone is insufficient and note the specific stage and sector characteristics that constrain peer relevance.
